lines: Merrin Underbough keeps the hearth-song low until the road is ready to hear itself.
See the story-quilt by the bench? Every square is a safe return, sewn before the brave part begins.
The verse keeps two promises: go out kindly, and come home with one more name than you left with.
If the road starts to roar, hum the second line and remember Hearthwake heard you first.
There now. The board has lent you its little courage, and courage is best returned with interest.
